reason: Locked requirements, acceptance criteria, definition of done — read first to understand WHAT to build.
Architecture, layered system view, decision log D1-D10 (all locked), implementation roadmap by phase. The HOW to build.
deepagents on LangChain 1.x is the chosen agent harness. Per-framework comparison + code sketches + LLMGateway design notes. Read before writing agent layer code.
MinerU 3.x is the PDF default; markitdown limited to non-PDF; GROBID optional. Verified failure modes of markitdown on scientific PDFs. Read before writing document pipeline.
babeldoc 0.6.2 exact API (do_translate_async_stream, event schema, TranslateResult), subprocess pattern, AGPL implications, model asset list. Read before writing translation worker.
Wiki directory contract, page templates from obsidian-paper-curator, slug + disambiguation rules ported from OpenSciReader, queries/ isolation discipline. Read before writing wiki tools.
Electron+Python sidecar architecture, spawn pattern, HTTP/WS contract, electron-builder packaging. Phase 3 reference (Phase 1-2 uses Vite browser tab dev mode).
Backend spec layer index. Currently stub (00-bootstrap-guidelines in progress) but sub-agents should know where to add Python-specific conventions as they emerge.
Frontend spec layer index. Currently stub; sub-agents should add React/Vite/shadcn conventions here as the UI codebase forms.
Cross-layer data flow discipline (FastAPI ↔ React, WS event protocols). Read when designing API contracts between backend and frontend.
